report.name: Office Paper Usage Reduction - DMAIC Attempt

categories.name: Define

attachments.name: paper_usage_observation.jpg
attachments.description: Basic observation of paper waste without systematic problem definition

categories.description: Informal problem identification without proper DMAIC project structure, business case, or systematic approach.

statements.name: Problem loosely identified as 'too much paper being used in the office'
No business case developed - cost impact unknown and unquantified
Project scope undefined - no clear boundaries or deliverables established
Customer requirements not identified or considered in problem definition
No formal project team - individual effort without Six Sigma expertise
No project charter, timeline, or management support obtained

categories.name: Measure

attachments.name: manual_paper_counting.jpg
attachments.description: Manual paper counting without systematic measurement approach

categories.description: Informal data collection without measurement system validation, baseline establishment, or statistical methodology.

statements.name: Basic paper counting performed inconsistently over 1 week period
No measurement system analysis - data accuracy and reliability unknown
No baseline establishment or trend analysis performed
Data collection plan informal and inconsistent across departments
No defect definition or quality metrics established
No statistical analysis or capability assessment attempted

categories.name: Analyze

attachments.name: simple_observation_notes.jpg
attachments.description: Handwritten notes from casual observation without analytical rigor

categories.description: Superficial analysis based on assumptions rather than data-driven investigation or statistical validation.

statements.name: Analysis based on casual observation rather than systematic investigation
No root cause analysis performed - causes assumed rather than validated
No statistical tools used - no Pareto, fishbone, or correlation analysis
Process mapping not performed - workflow and waste sources unknown
No hypothesis development or testing methodology applied
Conclusions reached without statistical validation or confidence assessment

categories.name: Improve

attachments.name: simple_reminder_signs.jpg
attachments.description: Basic reminder signs posted without systematic solution development

categories.description: Ad-hoc solutions implemented without systematic evaluation, pilot testing, or effectiveness validation.

statements.name: Solutions based on common sense rather than analytical findings
No pilot testing or solution validation performed before implementation
No risk assessment or mitigation planning conducted
Implementation plan informal without timeline or responsibility assignment
No change management or stakeholder communication performed
Results unmeasured - effectiveness unknown and unvalidated

categories.name: Control
categories.description: No control phase implementation - no monitoring, standardization, or sustainment planning established.

statements.name: No monitoring system established to track ongoing performance
No process capability assessment or improvement validation performed
No procedures documented or standardized for sustaining improvements
No training provided to ensure consistent application of new methods
No response plan for performance degradation or problem recurrence
No sustainment verification - project abandoned without follow-through

highlightsData.title: (Prepare)
highlightsData.description:
No Project Preparation: No formal project preparation attempted. Lack of project charter, executive sponsorship, or resource commitment. Team formation inadequate with no Six Sigma expertise or systematic project management approach.

Training Need: Fundamental Six Sigma methodology training required before attempting any structured improvement project. Yellow Belt certification recommended as foundational step.

highlightsData.title: Define
highlightsData.description:
Poor Problem Definition: Problem loosely identified as 'too much paper being used in the office' without systematic problem definition. No business case developed with cost impact unknown and unquantified.

Scope Undefined: Project scope undefined with no clear boundaries or deliverables established. Customer requirements not identified or considered in problem definition approach.

highlightsData.title: Measure
highlightsData.description:
Inadequate Measurement: Basic paper counting performed inconsistently over 1 week period without systematic measurement approach. No measurement system analysis with data accuracy and reliability unknown.

No Baseline: No baseline establishment or trend analysis performed. Data collection plan informal and inconsistent across departments with no statistical methodology applied.

highlightsData.title: Analyze
highlightsData.description:
No Analysis Performed: Analysis based on casual observation rather than systematic investigation. No root cause analysis performed with causes assumed rather than validated through data analysis.

No Statistical Tools: No statistical tools used including Pareto, fishbone, or correlation analysis. Process mapping not performed leaving workflow and waste sources unknown.

highlightsData.title: Improve
highlightsData.description:
Ad-Hoc Solutions: Solutions based on common sense rather than analytical findings. No pilot testing or solution validation performed before implementation with no risk assessment conducted.

Unmeasured Results: Implementation plan informal without timeline or responsibility assignment. Results unmeasured with effectiveness unknown and unvalidated.

highlightsData.title: Control
highlightsData.description:
No Control Phase: No monitoring system established to track ongoing performance. No process capability assessment or improvement validation performed leaving sustainment completely unaddressed.

Project Abandoned: No procedures documented or standardized for sustaining improvements. Project abandoned without follow-through or any systematic approach to performance maintenance.

actionItems.title: Enroll Team in Six Sigma Yellow Belt Training
actionItems.description: Provide fundamental Six Sigma methodology training including DMAIC framework, basic statistical tools, and project management principles. Include hands-on exercises and mentorship support for skill development.

actionItems.title: Restart Project with Proper DMAIC Structure
actionItems.description: Begin new project using proper Six Sigma methodology. Develop formal project charter, conduct stakeholder analysis, and establish clear problem statement with quantified business case. Engage experienced mentor for guidance.

actionItems.title: Establish Basic Measurement and Data Collection System
actionItems.description: Implement systematic data collection with defined metrics, measurement procedures, and baseline establishment. Include basic statistical analysis training and data validation methodology.
